panel.title: 🔒 Server Verification
panel.description:
**Welcome!** This server requires manual verification to help fight bot abuse.

React with ✅ below to begin the verification process.

You will be given a private channel with a captcha image — simply type the 6 letters shown to verify yourself.

setupComplete.title: ✅ Verification Setup Complete
setupComplete.description:
**Category:** Verification
**Panel Channel:** <#{panelChannelId}>
**Verified Role:** <@&{verifiedRoleId}>

Users can now react with ✅ in <#{panelChannelId}> to begin verification.

> **Important:** Make sure to deny `@everyone` the `View Channel` permission on your other channels, and allow the **Verified** role to see them. This ensures unverified users can only see the verification channel.

captcha.title: 🔒 Verification Required
captcha.description:
Welcome, <@{userId}>!

Please type the **6 letters** shown in the image above to verify yourself.

You have **{maxAttempts}** attempt(s). The code is **not** case-sensitive.
This channel will expire in **60 seconds**.

errors.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
errors.missingPanelChannel: Please specify a channel or run `{prefix}verify setup` first.
errors.usageTestFirst: Run `{prefix}verify setup` first to create the verification category.
errors.createTestVerificationChannelFailed: Failed to create test verification channel: {error}
errors.updateFailed: An error occurred while updating verification settings.
errors.invalidRole: Please mention a valid role or provide a role ID.
errors.invalidCategory: Please provide a valid category ID.
errors.invalidChannel: Please mention a valid channel or provide a channel ID.

verification.resetDone: Verification settings have been reset. Channels and roles created during setup were **not** deleted — remove them manually if needed.

result.passedDescription: You have been verified. This channel will be deleted shortly.
result.failedDescription: You have used all your attempts. This channel will be deleted. Please try again by reacting to the verification panel.
result.incorrectDescription: That wasn't right. You have **{remaining}** attempt(s) left.

help.title: Manual Verification System
help.body:
`{prefix}verify setup` - auto-create category, channel, role, and panel
`{prefix}verify panel [#channel]` - (re-)post the verification panel
`{prefix}verify role <@role>` - set the verified role
`{prefix}verify category <id>` - set the verification category
`{prefix}verify log <#channel>` - set a log channel
`{prefix}verify status` - show current configuration
`{prefix}verify reset` - disable and clear settings
`{prefix}verify test` - open a test verification channel for yourself

invite.title: Invite Fluxy
invite.description:
Add Fluxy to your server and get powerful moderation, welcome messages, logging, and more!

**[Click here to invite Fluxy]({inviteUrl})**

Need help? Join the **[Fluxy Support Server](https://fluxer.gg/fluxy)**.

myprefix.current: Your personal prefix is `{prefixValue}`. Use `{prefix}myprefix clear` to remove it.
myprefix.notSet: You don't have a personal prefix set. Use `{prefix}myprefix <prefix>` to set one.
myprefix.fetchFailed: Could not fetch your prefix setting.
myprefix.cleared: Your personal prefix has been removed. You will use the server default.
myprefix.saved: Your personal prefix has been set to `{newPrefix}`. You can now use `{newPrefix}help` anywhere.

mydata.info:
**Data Fluxy stores about you:**
- Personal prefix (if set via `!myprefix`)
- Warnings you have received in servers
- Moderation actions involving you (as target or moderator)
- Ticket messages you sent in ticket channels
- Command usage statistics
- Global ban entry (if applicable)

`{prefix}mydata export` - get a file with all your data
`{prefix}mydata delete` - permanently delete all your data
mydata.collecting: Collecting your data... I will DM you the export file.
mydata.dmContent: Here is all the data Fluxy has stored about you. This includes warnings, moderation logs, ticket participation, and settings.
mydata.dmOpenFailed: I could not open a DM with you. Make sure your DMs are open.
mydata.dmSendFailed: I could not send you a DM. Make sure your DMs are open and try again.
mydata.exportFailed: Something went wrong while collecting your data. Please try again later.
mydata.deleteConfirm:
**Are you sure?** This will permanently delete:
- Your personal prefix
- All your warnings across every server
- Your identity in moderation logs (anonymized to "[deleted]")
- Your identity in ticket transcripts (anonymized to "Deleted User")
- Your command usage statistics

**This cannot be undone.**
Run `{prefix}mydata delete` again within 60 seconds to confirm.
mydata.deleteNoData: No data was found to delete.
mydata.deleteFailed: Something went wrong while deleting your data. Please try again later.
mydata.unknown: Unknown option. Use `{prefix}mydata info`, `{prefix}mydata export`, or `{prefix}mydata delete`.

massban.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
massban.usage:
**Usage:** `{prefix}massban <startMessageId> <endMessageId> [reason]`
Bans all unique authors of messages between the two boundary messages (inclusive) in the current channel.
**Tip:** Right-click a message → Copy ID to get message IDs.
massban.invalidMessageIds: Please provide valid message IDs (17-22 digit numbers).
massban.couldNotDetermineChannel: Could not determine the current channel.
massban.couldNotFindStartMessage: Could not find start message with ID `{startId}` in this channel.
massban.couldNotFindEndMessage: Could not find end message with ID `{endId}` in this channel.
massban.statusFetching: ⏳ Fetching messages between the two boundaries...
massban.noBannableUsersFound: No bannable users found between those messages (bots and yourself are excluded).
massban.confirmDescription:
Found **{authorCount}** unique user(s) across **{messageCount}** messages.

**Reason:** {reason}

React with ✅ to confirm or ❌ to cancel.
*This will expire in 30 seconds.*
massban.confirmationReactionsFailed: Could not add confirmation reactions. Make sure I have **Add Reactions** permission.

setstaff.channelCleared: Staff output channel has been cleared.

setstaff.channelSetDone: Staff output channel set to <#{channelId}>. Reports from `{prefix}report` and the inbox channel will be forwarded there.
setstaff.roleCleared: Staff role ping has been cleared. Reports will still be posted but no role will be pinged.

setstaff.roleSetDone: Staff role set to <@&{roleId}>. They will be pinged on every new report.
setstaff.reportChannelCleared: Report inbox channel has been cleared. The bot will no longer monitor that channel.
setstaff.reportChannelInvalid: Please mention a valid channel or provide a channel ID.
setstaff.reportChannelSetDone:
Report inbox channel set to <#{channelId}>.
Any message sent there will be silently deleted and forwarded to the staff output channel.

**Recommended channel permissions for <#{channelId}>:**
- `@everyone`: Send Messages = on, Read Message History = **off**, View Channel = on
- Staff role: all permissions as normal
This way members can submit a report but cannot read what others have sent.
setstaff.unknownSubcommand: Unknown subcommand. Use `{prefix}setstaff status`, `{prefix}setstaff channel`, `{prefix}setstaff role`, or `{prefix}setstaff reportchannel`.

errors.generic: An error occurred while updating staff settings.

Blacklisted channels prevent the bot from responding to non-staff members.

ticket.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
ticket.openNotice: Tickets are now created by reacting to the ticket panel. Ask an admin to run `{prefix}ticket setup channel create` if no panel is set up yet.
ticket.missingManageServerPermission: You need **Manage Server** permission to configure tickets.
ticket.processingError: An error occurred while processing the ticket command.

access.couldNotVerifyPermissionsRightNow: Could not verify your permissions right now. Please try again.
access.supportRoleOrManageServer: You need a **support role** or **Manage Server** permission to manage tickets.

help.body:
**Ticket System**
`{prefix}ticket claim` - claim this ticket (shows you're handling it)
`{prefix}ticket close [reason]` - close the current ticket
`{prefix}ticket add <@user>` - add a user to the ticket
`{prefix}ticket remove <@user>` - remove a user from the ticket
`{prefix}ticket panel [#channel]` - post the ticket reaction panel
`{prefix}ticket setup` - configure the ticket system (admin)

*To create a ticket, react to the ticket panel message in the designated channel.*

category.usage:
Usage: `{prefix}ticket setup category <categoryId>`
Provide the ID of an existing category channel, or `create` to make one.
category.invalidCategoryId: Please provide a valid category channel ID.

channel.setupFirst: Set up a ticket category first: `{prefix}ticket setup category <id|create>`
channel.created: Ticket channel created: <#{channelId}> - users can now react to create tickets!
channel.invalidChannelId: Please mention a valid channel or provide a channel ID.
channel.botPermissionUpdateFailed: I couldn't update my permissions in <#{channelId}>. Make sure I have **Manage Channels** or manually grant me **Send Messages** and **Add Reactions** in that channel.
channel.panelPosted: Ticket panel posted in <#{channelId}> - users can now react to create tickets!

role.noSupportRoles:
No support roles configured.

`{prefix}ticket setup role add <@role>` - add a support role
`{prefix}ticket setup role remove <@role>` - remove a support role
`{prefix}ticket setup role clear` - remove all support roles
role.invalidRoleId: Please mention a valid role or provide a role ID.
role.added: <@&{roleId}> added as a ticket support role. ({total} total)
role.removed: <@&{roleId}> removed from ticket support roles. ({remaining} remaining)
role.generalListUsage:
Usage:
`{prefix}ticket setup role add <@role>` - add a support role
`{prefix}ticket setup role remove <@role>` - remove a support role
`{prefix}ticket setup role clear` - remove all
`{prefix}ticket setup role` - list current roles

log.invalidChannelId: Please mention a valid channel or provide a channel ID.

max.usage: Usage: `{prefix}ticket setup max <1-10>` - max open tickets per user.
max.setDone: Max open tickets per user set to **{maxOpen}**.

message.usage:
Usage: `{prefix}ticket setup message <text>` or `{prefix}ticket setup message clear`
Variables: `{user}` `{server}` `{ticket}`

claim.ticketNotOpen: This command can only be used inside an open ticket channel.
claim.alreadyClaimed: This ticket is already claimed by <@{claimedBy}>.

close.ticketNotOpen: This channel is not an open ticket. Use this command inside a ticket channel.

add.ticketNotOpen: This command can only be used inside an open ticket channel.
add.added: <@{userId}> has been added to this ticket.

remove.ticketNotOpen: This command can only be used inside an open ticket channel.
remove.openedByCannotRemove: You cannot remove the person who opened the ticket.
remove.removed: <@{userId}> has been removed from this ticket.

panel.ticketNotSetup: Ticket system is not set up. Run `{prefix}ticket setup category <id|create>` first.
panel.channelNotFound: Could not find the target channel.
panel.posted: Ticket panel posted in <#{channelId}> - users can react to create tickets!

panelEmbed.title: Support Tickets
panelEmbed.description:
Need help? React with {emoji} below to create a ticket!

A private channel will be created for you and a staff member will assist you as soon as possible.

*You can only create one ticket every 10 minutes.*
panelEmbed.footer: React below to open a ticket

user.defaultOpenMessage:
Welcome <@{userId}>! A staff member will be with you shortly.

Use `{prefix}ticket close` to close this ticket when your issue is resolved.
user.createdNotice: Your ticket has been created! Head over to <#{channelId}> (ticket #{ticketNumber}).

reasons.maxOpenTickets: You already have **{openTickets}** open ticket(s). Maximum is **{maxOpen}**. Please close an existing ticket first.
reasons.waitCooldown: Please wait **{remaining}** before creating another ticket.

claimEmbed.title: Ticket #{ticketNumber} - Claimed
claimEmbed.description: <@{userId}> has claimed this ticket and will be handling it.

dmEmbed.description: Your ticket in **{guildName}** was closed by <@{userId}>.

setupEmbed.title: Ticket System Configuration
setupEmbed.setupCommands:
`{prefix}ticket setup category <id|create>` - set ticket category
`{prefix}ticket setup channel [#channel|create]` - set/create the ticket reaction channel
`{prefix}ticket setup role add/remove <@role>` - manage support roles
`{prefix}ticket setup log <#channel>` - set transcript log channel
`{prefix}ticket setup max <1-10>` - max open tickets per user
`{prefix}ticket setup message <text>` - custom open message ({user} {server} {ticket})

automod.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
automod.errorGeneric: There was an error executing this command.

levelDescriptions.minimal: Anti-spam on, anti-link off, relaxed mention/line limits
levelDescriptions.medium: Anti-spam + anti-link + anti-reaction spam on, moderate limits
levelDescriptions.high: Anti-spam + anti-link + anti-reaction spam on, strict limits

status.title: Automod Settings - {guildName}
status.spamThresholdsValue:
**Max messages:** {maxMessages} per **{timeWindow}s** window
**Timeout after:** {violationThreshold} violations -> {timeoutDuration} min timeout

spam.usage:
Usage: `!automod spam <messages|window|timeout|violations> <value>`
- `messages` - max messages per window (2-20, default 5)
- `window` - tracking window in seconds (1-60, default 5)
- `timeout` - timeout length in minutes (1-60, default 10)
- `violations` - violations before timeout (1-10, default 3)
spam.updated:
Spam settings updated.
Max **{maxMessages}** messages per **{timeWindow}s** -> timeout after **{violationThreshold}** violations (**{timeoutDuration} min**).

link.usage:
Usage:
`!automod link allow <domain>` - exempt a domain from the link filter
`!automod link deny <domain>` - remove a domain exemption

Example: `!automod link allow youtube.com`
link.alreadyAllowed: `{domain}` is already in the allowed list.
link.added: `{domain}` added to the link allowlist. Links from this domain will no longer be deleted.
link.notAllowed: `{domain}` is not in the allowed list.

reactionspam.activatedMinimal: Automod level set to `minimal` to activate it.

ghostping.noteLevelOff: **Note:** Automod level is `off` - run `!automod level minimal` for this to take effect.

exempt.usage:
Usage:
`!automod exempt role add <@role>` - exempt a role from all automod checks
`!automod exempt role remove <@role>` - remove role exemption
`!automod exempt channel add <#channel>` - disable automod in a channel
`!automod exempt channel remove <#channel>` - re-enable automod in a channel
exempt.invalidRole: Please provide a valid role mention or role ID.
exempt.roleNowExempt: Members with <@&{roleId}> are now exempt from automod.
exempt.roleNotExempt: That role is not in the exempt list.
exempt.roleNoLongerExempt: <@&{roleId}> is no longer exempt from automod.
exempt.invalidChannel: Please provide a valid channel mention or channel ID.
exempt.channelNotExempt: That channel is not in the exempt list.

help.title: Automod - Subcommands
help.fieldLevelValue:
`off` - disable all
`minimal` - anti-spam only
`medium` - anti-spam + anti-link
`high` - anti-spam + anti-link, strict limits
help.fieldLinkValue:
Manage domains exempt from the link filter
Example: `!automod link allow youtube.com`
help.fieldReactionSpamValue: Enable or disable anti-reaction spam detection
help.fieldExemptRoleValue: Exempt a role from all automod checks
help.fieldExemptChannelValue: Disable automod in a specific channel

setup.title: ⭐ Starboard Channel Set

help.title: Reaction Role Commands
help.fieldPostValue: Bot posts a reaction role panel in the channel
help.fieldAddValue:
Map an emoji to a role on a message
Paste the emoji directly or use a shortcode - find emojis at [getemoji.com](https://getemoji.com)
help.fieldAddMultiValue: Map an emoji to **multiple roles** at once (up to 10)
help.fieldEditValue: Edit the text of an existing reaction role panel
help.fieldClearValue: Remove all reaction roles from a message and reset it to 0/20
help.fieldListValue: List all configured reaction role panels
help.fieldDmValue: Toggle DM notifications when roles are assigned/removed
help.fieldSwitchValue: Map an emoji to switch roles: removes one role and adds another
help.footer: Tip: Copy emoji characters directly from getemoji.com - shortcodes like :white_check_mark: also work

common.channelNotFoundDetailed: Channel not found. Mention it, use its ID, or type its name.
common.invalidEmoji: Invalid emoji. Use the emoji directly (paste it), a shortcode (`:white_check_mark:`), or a custom server emoji (`<:name:id>`).
common.messageNotFoundDetailed: Message not found in that channel. Double-check the message ID and channel.
common.maxPairs: This panel already has the maximum of 20 emoji-role pairs.
common.alreadyMapped: {emojiArg} is already mapped on that message.
common.reactPermissionError: I couldn't add the {emojiArg} reaction to that message. Check that the emoji is valid and I have **Add Reactions** permission in <#{channelId}>.

post.success:
Panel posted in <#{channelId}>.
Message ID: `{messageId}`

To add roles to this panel, use:
`{prefix}rr add #{channelName} {messageId} <emoji> <@role>`

**Example:** `{prefix}rr add #{channelName} {messageId} ⭐ @Member`

add.invalidRole: Invalid role. Mention a role, provide its ID, or type the role name.
add.roleNotFound: Role not found. Make sure the role exists and the bot can see it.

edit.usage:
Usage: `{prefix}rr edit <#channel> <messageId> [new text]`
Leave text empty to be prompted for it.
edit.panelNotFound: No reaction role panel found for that message. Use `{prefix}rr list` to see configured panels.
edit.missingTextPrompt:
Please provide the new text for the panel.
Usage: `{prefix}rr edit #{channelName} {messageId} Your new panel text here`

Tip: You can use markdown formatting like **bold**, *italic*, __underline__, and ~~strikethrough~~.

remove.noneConfigured: No reaction roles configured for that message.
remove.mappingNotFound: No mapping found for {emojiArg} on that message.

clear.noneFound: No reaction roles found for that message ID. Use `{prefix}rr list` to see configured panels.
clear.done: Cleared {count} reaction role(s) from that message. You can now re-add up to 20.

list.noneConfigured: No reaction role panels configured in this server.
list.title: Reaction Role Panels
list.footer: DM Notifications: {dmStatus} - use "{prefix}rr dm on/off" to toggle

dm.done: DM notifications for reaction roles are now **{toggle}**.

switch.usage:
Usage: `{prefix}rr switch <#channel> <messageId> <emoji> <@removeRole> <@addRole>`
When a user reacts, the bot will **remove** the first role and **add** the second.
switch.invalidRemoveRole: Invalid remove role. Mention a role, provide its ID, or type the role name.
switch.invalidAddRole: Invalid add role. Mention a role, provide its ID, or type the role name.
switch.messageNotFound: Message not found in that channel.
switch.addRoleNotFound: Add-role not found. Make sure the role exists and the bot can see it.
switch.removeRoleNotFound: Remove-role not found. Make sure the role exists and the bot can see it.
switch.alreadyMappedRemoveFirst: {emojiArg} is already mapped on that message. Remove it first with `{prefix}rr remove`.
switch.done: Switch mapped: {emojiArg} -> remove **{removeRoleName}**, add **{addRoleName}** on that message.

addmulti.usage:
Usage: `{prefix}rr addmulti <#channel> <messageId> <emoji> <@role1> <@role2> ...`
Assign **multiple roles** with one reaction (minimum 2 roles, max 10).
addmulti.invalidRole: Invalid role: `{roleArg}`. Mention a role, provide its ID, or type the role name.
addmulti.roleNotFound: Role not found: `{roleId}`. Make sure the role exists and the bot can see it.
addmulti.alreadyMappedRemoveFirst: {emojiArg} is already mapped on that message. Remove it first with `{prefix}rr remove`.
addmulti.done: Done! {emojiArg} is now mapped to **{roleCount} roles**: {roleList}.

slowmodeperms.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
slowmodeperms.usage:
**Slowmode Permissions**
Allow specific roles to use `{prefix}slowmode` without needing Manage Channels permission.

`{prefix}slowmodeperms add <@role>` - allow a role to use slowmode
`{prefix}slowmodeperms remove <@role>` - remove a role from the allowlist
`{prefix}slowmodeperms list` - show allowed roles
`{prefix}slowmodeperms clear` - clear all (only Manage Channels users can use slowmode)
slowmodeperms.roleRequiredUsageAdd: Please specify a role. Usage: `{prefix}slowmodeperms add <@role>`
slowmodeperms.roleRequiredUsageRemove: Please specify a role. Usage: `{prefix}slowmodeperms remove <@role>`
slowmodeperms.invalidRole: Invalid role. Please use a role mention or role ID.
slowmodeperms.roleDoesNotExist: That role doesn't exist in this server.
slowmodeperms.alreadyAllowed: **{roleName}** is already in the slowmode allowlist.
slowmodeperms.added: Added **{roleName}** to the slowmode allowlist. Members with this role can use `{prefix}slowmode` without Manage Channels permission.
slowmodeperms.notInAllowlist: That role is not in the slowmode allowlist.
slowmodeperms.noteAllowlistEmpty: The allowlist is now empty - only users with Manage Channels can use slowmode.
slowmodeperms.noAllowOverrides: No slowmode role overrides are set. Only users with Manage Channels permission can use `{prefix}slowmode`.
slowmodeperms.noOverridesToClear: No slowmode role overrides to clear.
slowmodeperms.clearedAll: Cleared all slowmode role overrides. Only users with Manage Channels permission can use `{prefix}slowmode`.

errors.generic: An error occurred while managing slowmode permissions.

commandperms.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
commandperms.usage:
**Command Permissions**
Restrict bot command usage to specific roles. When set, only members with an allowed role (or staff/admins) can use non-admin commands.

`{prefix}commandperms add <@role>` - allow a role to use commands
`{prefix}commandperms remove <@role>` - remove a role from the allowlist
`{prefix}commandperms list` - show allowed roles
`{prefix}commandperms clear` - clear all (everyone can use commands)
commandperms.roleRequiredUsageAdd: Please specify a role. Usage: `{prefix}commandperms add <@role>`
commandperms.roleRequiredUsageRemove: Please specify a role. Usage: `{prefix}commandperms remove <@role>`
commandperms.invalidRole: Invalid role. Please use a role mention or role ID.
commandperms.roleDoesNotExist: That role doesn't exist in this server.
commandperms.alreadyAllowed: **{roleName}** is already in the command allowlist.
commandperms.added: Added **{roleName}** to the command allowlist. Members with this role can use bot commands.
commandperms.notInAllowlist: That role is not in the command allowlist.
commandperms.noteAllowlistEmpty: The allowlist is now empty - everyone can use commands again.
commandperms.noRestrictions: No command role restrictions are set. Everyone can use bot commands.
commandperms.noRestrictionsToClear: No command role restrictions to clear.
commandperms.clearedAll: Cleared all command role restrictions. Everyone can use bot commands again.

errors.generic: An error occurred while managing command permissions.

keywords.serverOnly: This command can only be used in a server.
keywords.showListEmpty:
**Keyword System** - {statusLine} | {actionLine}

No keywords configured. Use `{prefix}keywords add <word>` to add one.
keywords.addUsage:
Usage:
`{prefix}keywords add <word>` - plain match
`{prefix}keywords add regex <pattern>` - regex match
keywords.regexPatternRequired: Please provide a regex pattern after `regex`.
keywords.regexRejected: Regex rejected: {reason} Simplify it or use a plain keyword instead.
keywords.maxKeywordsReached: Maximum of {max} keywords reached. Remove one first.
keywords.duplicatePattern: That pattern is already in the list.
keywords.added:
Added {tag}: `{pattern}`

Don't forget to `{prefix}keywords enable` if the system is off.
keywords.removeInvalidNumber: Please give a valid number (1–{max}). Use `{prefix}keywords list` to see numbers.
keywords.actionWarn: Issue a warning but leave the message.
keywords.actionDelete: Silently delete the message, no warning.
keywords.actionDeleteWarn: Delete the message and issue a warning.
keywords.testMissingText: Provide some text to test: `{prefix}keywords test <your text here>`
keywords.unknownSubcommand: Unknown subcommand. Use `{prefix}keywords` to see all options.

Prefix: **`{prefix}`**
Use `{prefix}help <command>` for detailed info on any command.
